channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India Banks: Depositor TDS Clarity Under New Tax Act 2025
item.description: The Income Tax Department has clarified the Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) mandate for 'banking companies' under the new Income Tax Act, 2025. This directive confirms that institutions previously covered under Section 51 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, will continue to adhere to the established TDS thresholds for interest income. The clarification resolves potential ambiguities arising from definitional changes in the new Act, ensuring uninterrupted compliance and safeguarding existing exemptions for depositors.

item.title: HDFC Bank Stock Falls 24%, Ex-Chair Denies Exit Blame as RBI Reassures
item.description: HDFC Bank's stock has seen significant pressure and market cap erosion. Former chairman Atanu Chakraborty denies his exit was the primary cause, pointing to other factors. Meanwhile, the Reserve Bank of India has reaffirmed the bank's strong financial footing, liquidity, and governance. However, wider economic challenges and regulatory changes continue to affect the banking sector.

item.title: Lido DAO Plans $20M LDO Buyback to Address Valuation Gap
item.description: Lido DAO plans a $20 million buyback of its LDO governance token using stETH reserves to address a steep valuation discount. The move targets a perceived gap between LDO's market price, down over 95% from its peak, and the protocol's solid fundamentals. This also shows the limits of on-chain liquidity for large trades, requiring off-chain execution and reflecting wider DeFi governance token valuation issues.

item.title: India's Renewables Boom Fuels Import Rise Amid Demand Surge
item.description: India's renewable energy share has climbed to 20.2% of electricity production, backed by substantial credit growth. However, surging domestic energy demand is increasing overall import dependency to 40.6%. This rise in reliance on imported oil and gas, despite moderating coal imports, exposes India to global price volatility and geopolitical risks, even as renewable targets are met early.

item.title: India's GSV Plans Precision Skills Center for 1,000 Graduates
item.description: Gati Shakti Vishwavidyalaya (GSV) will create a precision manufacturing center of excellence, directed by Union Railways Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw to train 1,000 graduates each year. With no funding limits, the center aims to build a crucial talent pool for India's growing electronics, marine, aviation, and railway industries. The goal is to match global standards from Germany, the U.S., and Japan, but it must overcome significant hurdles in closing the country's skill gaps and competing with existing training programs.

item.title: NHAI Raises ₹28,307 Cr for New Projects, Nears ₹30K Cr FY26 Goal
item.description: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) is nearing its ₹30,000-crore asset monetisation target for FY26, having already raised ₹28,307 crore. This achievement comes from Infrastructure Investment Trust (InvIT) and Toll-Operate-Transfer (TOT) deals, which help fund new infrastructure projects by selling operational assets. This strategy frees up capital for new construction and to pay down debt, with recent InvIT sales showing strong investor interest.

item.title: Gold Loan Surge Masks Weakness in Traditional Indian Credit
item.description: Indian banks are seeing a clear split in credit. Gold loans have jumped 128% year-on-year to ₹4.29 lakh crore, fueled by demand for cash and higher gold prices. This surge stands in sharp contrast to a 14% drop in export credit and a 10% fall in consumer loans, which make up only a small fraction of total bank lending. Despite these specific drops, overall non-food bank credit grew a healthy 14.3% year-on-year. This growth was mainly supported by strong lending to the industry (13.5% rise) and services (16.3% rise) sectors. The trend shows a major change in how people borrow, with households increasingly using gold as collateral amid evolving economic conditions.

item.title: India's Tax Department Clarifies Bank Interest TDS Rules
item.description: India's Income Tax Department has clarified rules for banks under the new Income Tax Act, 2025, requiring Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) on interest income above set limits. Ordinary citizens have a Rs 50,000 threshold, senior citizens Rs 1 lakh. This aims to formalize income and boost tax collection.

item.title: Tejas Networks Shares Fall 5.9% Despite Major BharatNet Router Delivery
item.description: Tejas Networks marked a key delivery milestone, shipping over 17,000 IP/MPLS routers for India's BharatNet Phase III project. Even with this operational success and a strong future order pipeline, the company's stock dropped 5.9%. This performance suggests market concerns are outweighing the positive news from infrastructure development, particularly given the stock's significant drop over the past year.

item.title: India's Green Ammonia Surge: Record Prices Set, But Execution Hurdles Remain
item.description: Six fertilizer companies have secured 6.7 lakh MT of annual Green Ammonia supply, aiming to save $2.5 billion in foreign exchange over 10 years by replacing imported grey ammonia. The Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI) facilitated a price discovery mechanism that yielded rates as low as ₹49.75/kg, significantly undercutting global benchmarks. This pivotal development under India's National Green Hydrogen Mission positions the country as a potential leader in the green ammonia market, though significant execution and cost-parity challenges remain.

item.title: NGT Scrutiny Fuels Higher Costs, Project Delays for India Infra
item.description: India's infrastructure sector is grappling with higher costs and operational challenges from tougher environmental rules. The National Green Tribunal's strict oversight, seen in directives on road building and waste management, means rising compliance expenses and possible project delays. Companies face greater risks from fines, longer schedules, and the need to build stronger environmental protections, affecting profits and future investment.

item.title: Aave V4 Launches to Bridge DeFi with Real-World Assets Amid Tensions
item.description: Aave's long-anticipated v4 upgrade has gone live on Ethereum, introducing a modular 'hub-and-spoke' architecture to facilitate lending against real-world assets (RWAs) and broaden DeFi's scope. The upgrade aims to attract institutional capital with greater flexibility and new market types without fragmenting liquidity. However, the launch follows months of internal governance disputes and contributor turnover, raising questions about operational coordination. Aave's market capitalization is around $1.5 billion, with Total Value Locked (TVL) near $23.8 billion, positioning it as a leader in decentralized lending.

item.title: Court: PMLA Law Trumps SARFAESI, RDB for Bank Debt Recovery
item.description: The Bombay High Court's Nagpur bench has ruled the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) takes priority over bank recovery laws, including SARFAESI and the RDB Act. This means the government has the primary claim on properties linked to 'proceeds of crime.' Consequently, banks' ability to recover dues from these assets, even if mortgaged, is significantly limited. The ruling overturns previous decisions favoring banks and emphasizes the PMLA's role in seizing illicit assets, not just collecting debts. While legitimate third parties can still seek help in Special Courts, this changes how banks approach asset-backed lending and financial crime enforcement.

item.title: India's Auto Sector Faces Major Cybersecurity Compliance Risks
item.description: India's auto sector must adopt mandatory cybersecurity standard AIS 189, modelled on UN R155. However, major challenges like a critical shortage of skilled professionals, high compliance costs, and immature supply chain cybersecurity threaten successful implementation. With fewer than 15% of Indian OEMs seriously preparing, meeting the 2027 deadline is a serious concern.

item.title: AI Drives Energy Efficiency, But Power Demand Adds Emissions
item.description: Artificial intelligence promises big efficiency gains in energy manufacturing and grid management, helping decarbonization efforts. However, AI's massive and growing energy consumption, often powered by fossil fuels, risks increasing emissions. This creates a significant challenge for the sector: harnessing AI for clean energy while ensuring AI itself is powered sustainably.

item.title: Burger Singh's Valuation Leaps to ₹520 Cr on Franchise Model
item.description: Burger Singh has secured ₹82 crore in a Series B funding round, valuing the homegrown QSR chain at ₹520 crore. This follows a robust financial performance in FY25, where revenue reached ₹117 crore, a significant jump from ₹78 crore in FY24. The company dramatically narrowed its net losses to ₹3.86 crore and achieved its first positive EBITDA in over five years. The capital infusion will fuel its 'franchise-first' expansion strategy, aiming to scale its presence by building robust operational platforms for entrepreneurs across India.

item.title: India Pumps Billions into R&D Amid Fierce Global Tech Race
item.description: India is investing heavily in innovation, launching the ₹50,000 crore Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) and a ₹1 lakh crore Research, Development, and Innovation (RDI) Scheme. Alongside expanding the Bharat 6G Alliance and new research programs, the country faces tough global competition and past challenges in execution. Success hinges on turning this funding into globally competitive innovations in a fast-moving tech race.

item.title: Jefferies Upgrades Coal India to Buy, Targets ₹506 on Demand Outlook
item.description: Jefferies has upgraded Coal India (CIL) to 'Buy', assigning a ₹506 target price. The upgrade comes despite a recent dip in Q3FY26 revenue and profit, driven by lower sales and e-auction rates. However, Coal India's expansion into renewables and critical minerals, coupled with expected strong summer power demand and higher global energy prices, bolsters its outlook.

item.title: Qodo Raises $70M to Build Trust in AI-Generated Code
item.description: Qodo has raised $70 million in Series B funding, led by Qumra Capital. The company aims to solve the growing distrust in AI-generated code, which 95% of developers don't fully trust. Qodo provides verification, testing, and governance to ensure enterprise software built with AI is reliable and secure.

item.title: RBI Pumps ₹84,582 Cr Into Banks Despite Large Cash Surplus
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) injected ₹84,582 crore of transient liquidity into the banking system through two variable rate repo (VRR) auctions on Monday. This operation, detailed by the central bank, aims to manage market liquidity and align short-term interest rates with the policy repo rate, even amidst an estimated surplus of ₹1.27 lakh crore as of March 27. The RBI's strategy involves precise interventions using VRR auctions to balance ongoing demands with the broader liquidity environment.

item.title: Diamond Power Infra Wins ₹44 Crore UGVCL Cable Order
item.description: Diamond Power Infrastructure has secured a ₹43.94 crore Letter of Intent from Uttar Gujarat Vij Company Limited for 453 km of cables, to be executed over seven months. While the order strengthens its backlog, the company's recent stock dip prompts a closer look at its execution, profitability, and competition, including larger players like Polycab India.

item.title: WTO Deadlock: E-commerce Moratorium Expires After 26 Years
item.description: The World Trade Organization's 14th Ministerial Conference ended without agreement on key issues. Notably, the e-commerce duty moratorium expired for the first time in 26 years. Deadlocks over digital trade, IP safeguards, and WTO reform show deep divisions between major economies like the US and Brazil. This failure questions the WTO's future and suggests a greater reliance on regional trade deals.

item.title: India Gas Order Boosts Fertilizers Amid Geopolitical Supply Shocks
item.description: India has activated the Natural Gas (Supply Regulation) Order, 2026, to tackle supply disruptions caused by the West Asia conflict. The order prioritizes gas for fertiliser plants, aiming to ensure about 80% of their previous consumption levels via enhanced procurement. This action seeks to protect food security and farm inputs, even as geopolitical tensions increase raw material costs and strain government finances.

item.title: Zimbabwe Boosts Power With 500MW Floating Solar at Kariba Dam
item.description: Zimbabwe is launching a 500MW floating solar project at the Kariba Dam to energize its power sector. Partnering with Green Hybrid Power and the Intensive Energy User Group, with support from Afreximbank, the initiative will use underused transmission lines. The project aims to boost power for industries, diversify the economy with aquaculture, and earn foreign currency through energy sales and carbon credits, tackling transmission issues and climate impacts for better energy security.

item.title: Micron India Plant to Reach 10% Global Output on AI Chip Demand Surge
item.description: Micron Technology will manufacture 10% of its global output at its new Indian facility. Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw confirmed the plant's rapid ramp-up is fueled by soaring demand from AI data centers. This strategic move significantly boosts India's semiconductor capabilities and its ambition to become a net exporter of electronics.

item.title: JSW MG Motor India Hikes Prices Up to 2% Amid Rising Costs
item.description: JSW MG Motor India is increasing prices by up to 2% across its vehicle lineup, effective April 1, 2026. The move aims to offset higher raw material and logistics expenses, aligning with a trend seen across the auto industry as companies battle cost volatility. The impact on JSW MG Motor India's competitive standing, especially for its electric vehicles, will be closely watched.

item.title: India Markets Reel: Oil Shock Fuels Stagflation Fears, Hits Bonds & Rupee
item.description: Indian government bonds experienced a sharp sell-off, pushing the 10-year yield to its highest level in over a year. The rupee depreciated significantly, breaching key psychological levels against the dollar. This market turmoil is directly linked to a surge in global crude oil prices, driven by escalating Middle East tensions, which is stoking inflation fears and complicating the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy outlook. The combined pressures are widening India's trade deficit and threatening fiscal consolidation efforts.
